description: worker/tests/test_nodes_decode.py: unconditional torch import breaks CI (no torch in base.txt)
context: P18-D20's committed real-path test (test_vaedeode_real_path_returns_pil_image) and its _MockVaeWithDecode.decode() helper both do a bare 'import torch' with no guard, inside worker/tests/test_nodes_decode.py -- collected by the default CI invocation (pytest worker/tests -v, ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1). base.txt deliberately excludes torch (confirmed: diffusers/transformers do not pull it transitively -- torch is only an optional 'extra' on diffusers). This test fails in CI the moment it runs, since CI's venv has no torch. Fix: guard with pytest.importorskip("torch") at the top of the test function, OR move the test into Group B's realcpu-marked suite instead (decide which; if moved, remove it from this file and note the move in the implementation report).

description: worker/nodes/arch/clip/qwen3.py + clip_l.py: fix tokenizer asset directory depth
context: Both files resolve tokenizer_dir as Path(__file__).parent.parent / "assets" / "<name>_tokenizer". From worker/nodes/arch/clip/{qwen3,clip_l}.py this resolves to worker/nodes/arch/assets/<name>_tokenizer, which does not exist -- confirmed via find on live repo. Real location is worker/assets/<name>_tokenizer (one level higher). worker/nodes/arch/clip/t5.py already uses .parent.parent.parent with an inline comment explaining the correction ('the plan originally specified parent.parent, but the actual asset layout places tokenizers at worker/assets/'); apply the identical fix to qwen3.py and clip_l.py: change .parent.parent to .parent.parent.parent in both files, and add the same explanatory comment t5.py has. Do not change t5.py itself -- it is already correct. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=0 (real mode) instantiation of Qwen2Tokenizer.from_pretrained(tokenizer_dir) and CLIPTokenizer.from_pretrained(tokenizer_dir) must resolve to an existing directory containing tokenizer_config.json.

description: worker/nodes/loader.py: LoadClip.execute() missing torch import causes NameError before dispatch
context: LoadClip.execute()'s real-mode branch calls module.load(model_id, torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16) but never imports torch anywhere in that method's scope -- confirmed by direct execution: NameError: name 'torch' is not defined, fires before dispatch even reaches arch_clip.get_module(). Every other real-mode function in this file (LoadVae.execute()'s loader_fn, _load_model_from_hf_directory, _load_clip_from_hf_directory) does its own local 'import torch' immediately before use, per this module's documented lazy-import convention. Add 'import torch' inside LoadClip.execute()'s real-mode branch (after the ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=='1' early return, before the torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16 reference), matching the placement convention used in LoadVae.execute(). This task's fix is what makes P904-A2's tokenizer-path fix observable in isolation -- this task's NameError currently fires first and masks P904-A2's OSError.

description: worker/nodes/sampler.py: EmptyLatent real path references unbound name ctx instead of self.ctx
context: EmptyLatent.execute()'s real-mode branch ends with: return {"latent": torch.randn(shape, dtype=torch.float32, device=ctx.device)}. execute() is an instance method; the NodeContext is available as self.ctx, not a bare ctx -- there is no local or enclosing ctx in scope. This is a plain NameError the instant the real branch is reached with a model input provided (confirmed by direct execution). Change device=ctx.device to device=self.ctx.device. This is the only change required in this task -- do not alter the surrounding arch dispatch or shape-computation logic, which is correct.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py + worker/worker_main.py: reconcile cancel_flag type contract (threading.Event vs list[bool])
context: zit.py's _make_callback() calls cancel_flag.is_set(); its docstring says cancel_flag 'is expected to be a threading.Event (ANVILML_DESIGN.md SS1550)'. worker_main.py instead constructs _cancel_flag: list[bool] = [False] (line 48) with _cancel_flag[0]=False/True assignments. list[bool] has no .is_set() -- AttributeError on the first real denoising step once D18c's invocation is reached. Fix worker_main.py only: replace _cancel_flag: list[bool] = [False] with _cancel_flag = threading.Event(); replace _cancel_flag[0]=False with _cancel_flag.clear(); replace _cancel_flag[0]=True with _cancel_flag.set(). Do not touch zit.py -- threading.Event is the documented design. ANVILML_DESIGN.md SS1550 was verified directly during this phase's authoring -- it still specifies threading.Event exactly as NodeContext's docstring shows; worker_main.py's list[bool] is confirmed the side that diverges. No doc update or escalation needed for this task.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py + sampler.py: loader_fn reads tokenizer/text_encoder off the wrong object
context: zit.py sample()'s loader_fn does: tokenizer=getattr(conditioning,"tokenizer",None); text_encoder=getattr(conditioning,"text_encoder",None). Conditioning (encoder.py) only has .positive/.negative -- those live on RealClip (the clip object LoadClip produces). Fix: add clip: Any = None to sample()'s signature (after conditioning, defaulted like the existing vae param, so the three mock-mode calls in test_arch_zit.py that omit it keep working unchanged); loader_fn reads tokenizer/text_encoder from clip instead of conditioning. Update Sampler.execute() (sampler.py): add SlotSpec("clip","CLIP") to INPUT_SLOTS, pass clip=inputs.get("clip") through. The two real-mode tests (test_sample_real_assembles_pipeline_via_cache, test_sample_real_invokes_pipeline_with_correct_args) should still pass a mock clip explicitly; update their docs/TESTS.md entries to match. Do NOT edit docs/ANVILML_DESIGN.md -- human-authored only.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py: remove vestigial vae parameter from sample()/loader_fn
context: zit.py sample() carries an unused-by-design vae: Any=None parameter, forwarded into loader_fn's ZImagePipeline(vae=vae,...) construction. Confirmed via diffusers 0.38.0 source: ZImagePipeline.__init__ accepts vae=None without raising (register_modules tolerates None; vae_scale_factor falls back to 8), and __call__ never dereferences self.vae when output_type="latent" (only at final decode, ~lines 583-586, which Sampler's call never reaches). Sampler must never receive or forward vae -- VaeDecode (P18-D20) is the sole owner. Remove the vae parameter entirely from sample()'s signature and the 'vae=vae' line from loader_fn's ZImagePipeline(...) call. Do not add a vae input to Sampler.INPUT_SLOTS. worker/tests/test_arch_zit.py passes vae=None and vae=mock_vae at two call sites -- remove both kwargs, since sample() no longer accepts vae; update the corresponding docs/TESTS.md entries to match.

description: worker/nodes/arch/clip/{qwen3,clip_l,t5}.py: text encoder models never moved to ctx.device, always run on CPU
context: None of the three load() functions call .to(device) on the constructed model after model.load_state_dict(...). RealClip is also never constructed with device= anywhere in the chain. Net effect: text encoding silently runs on CPU even when ctx.device is cuda:0, no exception raised. Fix: widen each load(model_id, torch_dtype) to load(model_id, torch_dtype, device: str = "cpu") -- defaulted to match RealClip's own existing default, so the three existing mock-mode tests calling load("/fake/path", None) positionally in each of test_arch_clip_{qwen3,l,t5}.py keep working unchanged; LoadClip.execute() passes device=self.ctx.device explicitly in real mode. Each load() calls model.to(device) before returning, constructs RealClip(tokenizer, model, device=device). Apply identically to qwen3.py, clip_l.py, t5.py. Do NOT edit docs/ANVILML_DESIGN.md -- human-authored only.

description: worker/nodes/loader.py: LoadModel and LoadVae real paths never move loaded components to ctx.device
context: Same defect class as P904-A8, broader scope: _load_model_from_hf_directory's ZImageTransformer2DModel.from_single_file(model_id, torch_dtype=torch.float16) and LoadVae.execute()'s AutoencoderKL.from_single_file(model_id, torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16) never reference self.ctx.device, pass no device_map, and never call .to(device) on the result -- both default to CPU placement. Fix: _load_model_from_hf_directory gains a device: str param (LoadModel.execute() calls it as _load_model_from_hf_directory(model_id, model_id, self.ctx.device)), calls transformer.to(device) before constructing RealModel. LoadVae.execute()'s loader_fn closure captures self.ctx.device, calls .to(device) on the AutoencoderKL result before returning. Verify the post-.to() return value is what gets used -- .to() returns a new reference for some wrappers and mutates in place for others depending on diffusers version; always assign the return value.

description: worker/nodes/loader.py: remove deprecated HF-directory loading remnants entirely
context: _load_from_hf_directory, _load_clip_from_hf_directory are dead code (never called, kept 'for future reactivation' per their own docstrings). Per explicit decision, retaining them for future use is reversed -- delete both functions entirely, plus their now-unused transformers imports (CLIPTextModelWithProjection, CLIPTokenizer, Qwen2Tokenizer, Qwen3ForCausalLM, T5ForConditionalGeneration, T5TokenizerFast) if not used elsewhere in this file after removal. Do not deprecate-and-keep -- delete outright. This is a pure removal task; do not rename or touch _load_model_from_hf_directory here, that is P904-A10's job, sequenced after this one specifically so the rename diff is not tangled with this deletion diff.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py: add load_transformer() — offline transformer loading, no HF network access
context: Root cause of confirmed HF network access during real LoadModel: ZImageTransformer2DModel.from_single_file() with no config=/original_config= falls through to fetch_diffusers_config(checkpoint), guessing an HF repo id and downloading its config.json (diffusers/loaders/single_file_model.py, v0.38.0). local_files_only is insufficient -- only skips download if the guessed repo is already cached. Fix: add load_transformer(model_id: str) -> ZImageTransformer2DModel to zit.py. Construct ZImageTransformer2DModel() with zero args -- defaults match published 6B ZiT config (dim=3840, n_layers=30, n_heads=30, cap_feat_dim=2560). Load raw checkpoint via safetensors.torch.load_file(model_id), remap keys via diffusers.loaders.single_file_utils.convert_z_image_transformer_checkpoint_to_diffusers(checkpoint) -- reuse diffusers' remap/QKV-defuse logic, don't reimplement -- then load_state_dict(remapped). Zero network calls. Private diffusers import, not public API -- may break on version bumps.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py: add load_vae() — offline VAE loading, no HF network access
context: Same root cause as P904-A10, for AutoencoderKL.from_single_file() (identical FromOriginalModelMixin path, confirmed via source). VAE bound to diffusion model per design decision -- lives in zit.py. Add load_vae(model_id: str) -> AutoencoderKL to zit.py. Construct AutoencoderKL(block_out_channels=[128,256,512,512], ...) using the published config already documented in this file's VAE_SCALE_FACTOR comment. Load raw checkpoint via safetensors.torch.load_file(model_id); remap keys via diffusers.loaders.single_file_utils.convert_ldm_vae_checkpoint(checkpoint, config) where config is a plain dict with down_block_types/up_block_types matching the 4-entry block_out_channels length -- this function only reads list length from config, not a YAML; do not build a YAML-shaped original_config. Function correctly handles both prefixed (first_stage_model./vae.) and bare standalone-file keys -- confirmed: empty-prefix fallback matches every key when no prefix is present.

description: worker/nodes/loader.py: rewrite LoadModel/LoadVae/LoadClip's loader functions as thin per-arch wrappers
context: loader.py has its own inline diffusers calls (ZImageTransformer2DModel.from_single_file in the old _load_model_from_hf_directory; AutoencoderKL.from_single_file inline in LoadVae.execute()) instead of dispatching through the arch system, unlike LoadClip which already correctly uses arch_clip.get_module(). Fix: rename _load_model_from_hf_directory to _load_model_from_safetensors; keep its existing safetensors-metadata arch-detection logic as-is (unrelated to the HF bug), replace the direct ZImageTransformer2DModel call with arch.diffusion.get_module_by_name(detected_arch).load_transformer(model_id) (P904-A10, P904-A13). LoadVae.execute()'s inline loader_fn becomes _load_vae_from_safetensors(model_id, arch), calling get_module_by_name(arch).load_vae(model_id) (P904-A11). LoadClip.execute()'s dispatch becomes a named _load_clip_from_safetensors(model_id, clip_type) wrapper for naming symmetry only -- no behavior change, already dispatches correctly via arch_clip.get_module().

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/__init__.py: add an arch-by-name lookup, since LoadModel/LoadVae dispatch before any model object exists
context: get_module(model_obj) requires an already-constructed object with .arch -- but LoadModel/LoadVae (P904-A12) only have a bare arch string (from safetensors metadata or a path-derived guess) at the point they need to dispatch, no model object yet. Add get_module_by_name(arch: str) -> ModuleType | None alongside the existing get_module(model_obj) -- same pkgutil.iter_modules() scan, but matches each module's can_handle() against a tiny shim object carrying only .arch=arch (can_handle() already only reads getattr(model_obj, 'arch', None), confirmed via zit.py source -- a bare shim with just that one attribute satisfies it without constructing a real model). Do not change can_handle()'s own signature or get_module(model_obj)'s existing behavior -- this is a pure addition, zero risk to already-passing callers.

description: worker/nodes/loader.py: LoadVae missing device arg (TypeError on first real call); LoadClip stale docstring
context: Confirmed at HEAD (commit b54f40b, post-A13): LoadVae.execute() calls _load_vae_from_safetensors(model_id, "zit") with only 2 of the function's 3 required positional args -- device is missing entirely (the function signature is _load_vae_from_safetensors(model_id: str, arch: str, device: str), no default on device). This raises TypeError: missing 1 required positional argument: 'device' the instant LoadVae's real path executes; confirmed via ast inspection, not assumed. LoadModel's equivalent call correctly passes self.ctx.device as the third argument -- LoadVae's doesn't. Fix: change the call to _load_vae_from_safetensors(model_id, "zit", self.ctx.device). Separately: LoadClip.execute()'s docstring still says 'Raises NotImplementedError... stubbed until P18-D1' -- stale, the real dispatch already works via _load_clip_from_safetensors (A12); update the docstring to match actual behavior, no code change needed there.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py: load_transformer() — replace diffusers-internals reuse with shape-inferred config + manual key remap
context: load_transformer() currently zero-arg-constructs ZImageTransformer2DModel then calls a diffusers internal (convert_z_image_transformer_checkpoint_to_diffusers) -- private, unversioned. Replace with direct shape inference (ComfyUI pattern). CONFIRMED against a full real-checkpoint key scan (sorted, untruncated, see attached scan.log): dim=3840 (attention.out.weight [3840,3840]), head_dim=128 (q_norm/k_norm.weight [128]), n_heads=n_kv_heads=30 (dim/head_dim), n_layers=30 (layers.0..29 present, confirmed max index), n_refiner_layers=2 (context_refiner and noise_refiner both 0..1), cap_feat_dim=2560 (cap_embedder.0.weight [2560]), in_channels=16 -- NOT 64 as an earlier draft claimed: final_layer.linear.weight is [64,3840], and 64 = patch_size**2 * f_patch_size * in_channels = 2**2*1*16, so in_channels must be derived by dividing the patch factors out, not read directly. Implement the qkv fused-QKV split directly (correct in the draft) without importing it from diffusers.

description: worker/nodes/arch/diffusion/zit.py: load_vae() — replace diffusers-internals reuse with shape-inferred config + manual key remap
context: load_vae() currently constructs AutoencoderKL(block_out_channels=[128,256,512,512]) then calls diffusers.loaders.single_file_utils.convert_ldm_vae_checkpoint -- same private-internal concern as P904-B1. CONFIRMED via full real VAE key scan: latent_channels=16 (decoder.conv_in.weight [512,16,3,3] dim 1, matches transformer's in_channels); block_out_channels=(128,256,512,512) (decoder.up.{0..3} channel progression, reversed order); in/out_channels=3 (encoder.conv_in/decoder.conv_out). layers_per_block=2 -- NOT 3: decoder.up.N.block has 3 resnets per stage, but diffusers' Decoder uses num_layers=layers_per_block+1 for up-blocks only (confirmed in vae.py source) -- apply this -1 offset, don't read 3 directly. scaling_factor (0.18215, SD1.x default) is unconfirmable from shapes -- not a stored weight; flag as best-effort, wrong value risks brightness error not a crash. Scan block count dynamically, don't hardcode 4 stages.

description: worker/nodes/loader.py: switch arch detection from safetensors-metadata-only to key-prefix-based detection
context: _load_model_from_safetensors currently detects arch by reading safetensors metadata (an 'arch' key written by the export tool, if present) with a path-derived fallback -- this works today but doesn't scale to multiple architectures whose checkpoints may not carry export-tool metadata at all. Add key-prefix-based detection as the primary signal, matching the ComfyUI pattern: inspect the raw state dict's key prefixes before any stripping (e.g. keys starting with model.diffusion_model. indicate ZiT; a future flux.py would add its own distinguishing prefix/key pattern). Keep the existing metadata-based detection as a fallback for checkpoints that do carry it, do not remove it -- this is additive, not a replacement of P904-A12's working dispatch flow. Confirm this doesn't change LoadModel's behavior for the currently-supported ZiT checkpoint (same detected_arch result, same get_module_by_name() dispatch).

description: worker/nodes/arch/clip/{qwen3,clip_l,t5}.py: confirm no rework needed — already fully offline
context: Confirmed by reading all three files: each constructs its model via Config(**pinned_values) + load_state_dict (no from_single_file, no network-capable config fetch) -- the diffusers-internals/HF-network concern P904-B1/B2 address does not apply here. The only from_pretrained() call loads the tokenizer from a local vendored directory under worker/assets/ (offline by path, not mechanism -- from_pretrained() can hit the network, but only given a repo id, never a local directory path, which is all these files ever pass). This is a verification close-out, not a code change: re-confirm both points still hold at ACT time (no version bump changed from_pretrained's local-path behavior; no pinned_values drifted from a real checkpoint's actual shapes), then record the confirmation in the implementation report. If either check fails, escalate rather than silently patching -- the task's premise would be wrong.

description: worker/tests/real_fixtures.py: synthetic tiny clip checkpoint fixtures (qwen3/clip_l/t5)
context: Real Qwen3-4B/CLIP-L/T5-XXL weights are multi-GB, not viable as test fixtures. Create worker/tests/real_fixtures.py with tiny_qwen3_clip / tiny_clip_l_clip / tiny_t5_clip pytest fixtures: construct each via its real transformers Config class with hidden_size=32, num_hidden_layers=2, save via safetensors.torch.save_file to tmp_path. These are unaffected by the P904-A9..A14 remap concern -- qwen3.py/clip_l.py/t5.py call load_state_dict() directly on whatever is saved with no key-remap step, so a model's own native state_dict() is exactly the right format to save. Each fixture returns a saved file path, not the model object. Imported by P904-Z1b, B3, B4, B5.

description: worker/tests/real_fixtures.py: raw-checkpoint-format ZiT transformer and VAE fixtures (pre-remap keys)
context: load_transformer()/load_vae() (P904-A10/A11) consume RAW checkpoint key formats (fused qkv.weight, model.diffusion_model. prefix) via diffusers' remap functions -- a fixture saving a model's own state_dict() would skip the remap/QKV-defuse path, giving false test confidence. Add tiny_zit_transformer_raw: build a tiny ZImageTransformer2DModel(dim=64, n_layers=2, n_heads=2, cap_feat_dim=64), invert its state_dict() into raw-checkpoint format before saving -- fuse to_q/to_k/to_v into qkv.weight via torch.cat, rename all_x_embedder.2-1./all_final_layer.2-1. back to x_embedder./final_layer., prepend model.diffusion_model. to every key. Build the inverse mapping from convert_z_image_transformer_checkpoint_to_diffusers's own rename table directly -- don't guess, the two must be exact inverses. Add tiny_vae_raw similarly, inverting convert_ldm_vae_checkpoint's DIFFUSERS_TO_LDM_MAPPING for a tiny AutoencoderKL(block_out_channels=(8,16), latent_channels=4).

description: pytest.ini + .github/workflows/ci.yml: register realcpu marker, exclude from CI
context: Register a marker so the new real-mode CPU suite is opt-in, never picked up by CI's default invocation (pytest worker/tests -v, ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1, .github/workflows/ci.yml ~line 80). Add to worker/tests/pytest.ini: markers = realcpu: real-mode CPU test, requires torch+diffusers+transformers, not run by default CI. Update ci.yml's worker job to add -m "not realcpu" to the pytest call as defense-in-depth (redundant with torch's absence from base.txt causing collection errors otherwise, but explicit is clearer). worker/requirements/cpu-linux-agent.txt (torch via the PyTorch CPU index, --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu) already exists on main -- creating it is OUT OF SCOPE for this task; only consume it as a given fact when documenting how P904-Z3..B5 are run (manual-install-only, matching the existing rocm-linux.txt/rocm-windows.txt convention -- do not wire it into install_worker_deps.sh).

description: worker/tests/test_real_loaders.py: real CPU node tests for LoadModel/LoadVae/LoadClip
context: Create worker/tests/test_real_loaders.py, all tests @pytest.mark.realcpu, using P904-Z1/B1b's fixtures, ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=0 forced per-test (same override pattern as test_arch_zit.py's test_sample_real_assembles_pipeline_via_cache). Assert on real outputs: LoadModel.execute(model_id=<tiny_zit_transformer_raw path>) returns a RealModel with correct .in_channels (4, the tiny config); LoadVae.execute(model_id=<tiny_vae_raw path>) returns a real AutoencoderKL; LoadClip works for qwen3/clip_l/t5, each placing the text encoder on ctx.device (verifies A7). Also add test_loadmodel_no_network_access: monkeypatch huggingface_hub's network entry points (e.g. hf_hub_download) to raise, then call LoadModel.execute() and assert it completes without triggering the guard -- this is the direct regression test for the A9-A13 HF-network-access fix, the defect this whole sub-effort exists to close.

description: worker/tests/test_real_encoder_sampler.py: real CPU node tests for ClipTextEncode/EmptyLatent/Sampler/VaeDecode
context: Create worker/tests/test_real_encoder_sampler.py, all tests @pytest.mark.realcpu, building on P904-Z3's loader outputs as direct inputs (no mocking), using B1/B1b's tiny fixtures. ClipTextEncode.execute(clip=<real RealClip>, text="a", negative_text="") returns Conditioning with .positive/.negative as non-empty lists of real torch.Tensor, last-dim matching B1's tiny hidden_size. EmptyLatent.execute(width=128, height=128, batch_size=1, model=<real RealModel>) returns a real torch.Tensor matching compute_latent_shape() (verifies A4). Sampler.execute(..., steps=1, cfg=1.0, seed=0) -- minimal load, no meaningful image needed -- returns unchanged-shape latent + resolved int seed (verifies A5, A6/A6b). VaeDecode.execute(vae=<real tiny_vae_raw>, latent=<matching tensor>) returns a real PIL.Image.Image (D20 committed after B1-B3 were originally scoped; first real-mode coverage for it, no A-group bugfix needed -- D20's formula verified correct against ZImagePipeline's own decode step).

description: worker/tests/test_real_chain.py: full LoadModel->LoadVae->LoadClip->ClipTextEncode->EmptyLatent->Sampler->VaeDecode chain on tiny CPU weights
context: Create worker/tests/test_real_chain.py, marked @pytest.mark.realcpu, one end-to-end test wiring all seven node outputs into the next node's inputs exactly as worker/executor.py's run_graph() would (do not call run_graph() itself -- construct and call each node directly in sequence, so the test is independent of executor.py's own correctness). Use B1/B1b's tiny fixtures, width=height=128, steps=1, single batch. Assert: no exception raised end-to-end; Sampler's output latent shape equals EmptyLatent's output shape; resolved seed is a non-negative int; final VaeDecode output is a real PIL.Image.Image, not a MockImage. The chain now extends through VaeDecode (P18-D20, committed after this task was originally scoped to stop at Sampler) -- this is the project's first test exercising the complete real-mode node graph, model load through decoded image, end-to-end.
